We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify as non-current assets, the portion of the receivables which do not conform to the criteria provided for in paragraph 76 of the IPSAS in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommend that management direct the Property Officer together with the Disposal Committee to process the documents/forms for disposal of unserviceable properties and submit to the COA Auditor pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant, upon disposal, drop the cost of the unserviceable/obsolete/unfound PPE from the books to reflect the accurate value of PPE in the financial statements.

We recommend that the municipality stop immediately the approval of payments of honoraria to the police for successful drug operations pursuant to the cited provisions of government laws, rules and regulations to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.
With the nature of the disbursement as a reward, consider using the funds for intelligence and confidential purposes since payment of reward is allowed in the said fund among others.

We recommend that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that  intended purpose  is attained/achieved as required in Section 1 of Rule 18,  Republic Act 10121.

We recommend that management ensure the full utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund and be guided by the afore-cited Joint Memorandum Circular. We also recommend that management with the assistance of the MPDC implement the projects identified within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials in order to address the needs of the community.
We recommend that management observe the guiding principles as prescribed under the Local Government Code and the DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circulars to formulate an updated Local Revenue Code.

We recommend that management provide a safe and adequate garage or motor pool for all motor vehicles and heavy equipment to extend the useful life of these assets. Also, we recommend that management dispose immediately those beyond repair following the guidelines on disposal to avoid further deterioration.

Require the Municipal Accountant to record all valid reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements immediately in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of the daily time records of each employee duly signed by their immediate supervisors to support payment of their salaries and wages and provide bundy clocks or biometric machine as mechanical control in their actual attendance and observance of office hours required per Civil Service rules and regulations.
observations.reason: For implementatio n and compliance

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management establish a Local Council for the Protection of Children and require appropriation of at least 1% of the IRA for the implementation of LCPC programs.

We recommend that management carefully review the budget/appropriation charged to SEF with emphasis on the expenses enumerated under Section 3.2 of the Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the DEPED, DBM and DILG.
We recommend that Management submit the lacking document and observe COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 on the documentation requirements of fund transfers.

We recommend that management comply with the procedures provided by COA Circular 92-386, NGAS Manual, and COA Revised Guidelines on Appraisal of property and prepare an Agency Appraisal report to facilitate proper evaluation and disposal of these unserviceable properties and waste materials the soonest possible time.
Also, we recommend that all unserviceable properties and waste materials should be properly recorded and accounted for.
We recommend management to stop referring to brand names in preparing Purchase Requests. Instead, mention the relevant specifications and/or performance requirements of the supplies/materials needed.

We recommend that Management:
Consolidate the requisitions from different offices to facilitate the conduct of appropriate procurement method, which is through the competitive public bidding. In cases where public bidding is impractical, the BAC, after a thorough deliberation, shall recommend the appropriate method of procurement to the Head of Procurement Entity or the local chief executive by way of a resolution; and
Follow the guidelines set by the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in case shopping is resorted into.

We have recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the timely submission of bank reconciliation statements for all the cash in bank accounts of the municipality and periodically recognize all reconciling items in the books as applicable.
It is to be enjoined that monthly bank snapshots may be obtained from the depository banks of the municipality or avail of their internet banking facilities should receipt of the official bank statements are evidently delayed.
To require the Municipal Treasurer to return all unexpended balances of fund transfers to source government agencies and to facilitate closure of all dormant bank accounts in accordance with prescribed rules, laws, and regulations.  Further, to prepare inventory of obsolete checks for inspection and subsequent disposal.

We have recommended to management to update or revise the financial plan and strategy of its various economic enterprises to effectively and efficiently utilize resources in not only extending basic services to its constituents but also contribute to the attainment of its development goals and objectives and whether these economic enterprises are viable and shall continue to operate.

We have recommended and management committed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval.

We have recommended and management agreed to secure from the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated a government official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.

We recommend that management:
Ensure the implementation of the program that will focus on providing assistance in times of crisis and emergency situations and provide opportunities that will ultimately enhance the indigent constituents financial self-reliance, by strictly adhering to the provisions of DSWD Department Order 22.
Require the submission of the basic supporting documents for all claims of financial assistance to ensure that only valid disbursements will be paid, pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No.1445.
Establish clear-cut policies and guidelines in the release of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICS) Program.
observations.managementAction: All AICS are supported with the necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the concerned Accountable Officers to liquidate immediately their cash advance in compliance with the provisions of Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Otherwise withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees notwithstanding the imposition of other sanctions provided for under Sections 9.2 and 9.3 of the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: Collections had been made. In fact, only ₱170,044.31 remains uncollected as of 9/30/17 for accounts which are 1 to more than 5 years old.
observations.reason: Unliquidated amount not yet fully collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: stop using government funds for advertising in newspapers, TV or radio for greeting to a certain public official on their birthdays/ anniversaries, etc. which is considered irregular and unnecessary based on the above rules and regulations.

We recommend that management require the HR-designate to establish a qualifying system of hiring the JO personnel which should basically include the following procedures:
Require the applicants to submit PDS, Birth Or Marriage Certificates, NBI or Police Clearance and other valid proofs of identity and suitability for the job being applied for;
Require applicants to undergo interview and other basic screening process; and
Evaluate the performance of JO personnel and determine at the end of the contract period whether there is still necessity of renewing the job orders.

We recommend that management prepare and implement projects charged under the 20% MDF that partake the nature of investment and capital expenditures, that contribute to the attainment of the desirable socio economic, development and environmental management outcomes as stated in the guidelines, in order to attain maximum benefits for which the fund was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: Management specified that the expenses were necessary to carry out the projects under the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management should undertake the following:

Plan and implement GAD Programs that will recognize the need to remove biases against women and special attention to women through affirmative action.
Management should implement GAD identified activities pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) and the National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women (NCRFW).

observations.recommendation: It is hereby recommended that Management terminate time deposit placements at private banks. If these funds are determined to be idle or excess funds, then they should instead be invested in government banks. We also recommend that the Auditor be furnished with authenticated copies of Certificate of Time Deposit and the SB Resolution authorizing such time deposit.
observations.managementAction: All time deposit are closed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit a written justification for the grant of PEI in excess of the amount authorized under Section 1 of EO No. 80 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3, which set the Guidelines on the Grant of the PEI. Otherwise, require refund of the excess amount by the officials and employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management justified the PEI given by the LGU was according to the guidelines set.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deduction from the payroll and terminate immediately the arrangement (MOA) with the FCB and with other private lending institutions. For regular employees who wish to avail of such loan facility, they may transact directly with the private lending institution concerned without involving the LGU personnel and resources to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses.
observations.managementAction: No more loan applications are approved or processed.
observations.reason: The remittances to FCB are from those who have availed of the loan before its suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit a written justification why the Annual Investment Plan of the 20% Development Fund included lump sum appropriations and clearly specify the priority projects and programs that shall be funded out of the 20% Development Fund in order to develop the appropriate monitoring of implementation of projects and to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: All utilizations supported with supplemental investment plan

observations.recommendation: Submit Schedule of Disallowances indicating the names of concerned employees and amount due them and check whether the staffs are still in service. Enforce prompt payment of refunds for the said disallowance through gradual deduction from whatever form of benefits/salaries received by the persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Complied and Collected

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption. Direct the vehicle drivers concerned to completely fill up or indicate the following data on the Driver’s Trip Ticket: Vehicle Plate Number, Time of departure and arrival of the trip, balance in tank at the beginning and end of the trip, distance traveled, odometer reading, if available, liters of gasoline purchased and consumed for the trip, place of destination, purpose of travel and name of the driver and passengers with their respective signatures. Direct or assign an employee responsible to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis and verification on fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: Complied the recommendations provided.

observations.recommendation: Conduct a massive and efficient tax collection operation by mobilizing collecting clerks to accelerate collection efficiency. Require the officials concerned to enforce collection of all real property taxes including tax overdues and penalties. Seek assistance from the barangay officials in explaining to the taxpayers the benefits that their barangays would get in form of increased barangay shares which could enhance small scale programs and activities. Require the Treasurer to update the list of delinquent Real Property taxpayers to easily identify and determine the amount of taxes due and demandable.
observations.managementAction: Corrected

observations.recommendation: Refrain from spending personal and unnecessary disbursements considering the observance of austerity measures promulgated under COA Circular 85-55A and Administrative Order No. 103. Utilize monthly commutable representation allowances given to the Municipal Officers and Employees for entertainment of guests doing their official function. Stop the grant of substantial food supplies to PNP personnel, except for army detachment, being government employees receiving salaries and subsistence allowance from the National Government.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: Terminate immediately the present collection arrangement and assign a duly designated permanent and bonded employee to undertake the collecting function. Stop the hiring of job-order employees performing collecting functions. Utilize the services of existing regular employees to handle collection function on wharfage and toll fees.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the municipal accountant to grant Cash Advance only to duly appointed or designated Disbursing Officer.

Reflect the real payee of the cash advance and not the municipal mayor as practiced. 

Instruct the Special Disbursing Officer and other concerned employees to fully liquidate at year-end their cash advance without exception.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA – Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 and COA – Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1992. And MC No. 2 & No. 15 series of 2014, DSWD
We have recommended the management to deduct only authorized deductions and to strictly follow the provisions of the aforementioned law.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the management should transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRMMF to the Special Trust Fund using the following entries:

General Fund Book	

Debit    Transfer of unspent LDRRMF

Credit	  Cash in Bank-LCCA

Trust Fund Book

Debit Cash in Bank-LCCA

Credit Trust Liability-DRRM

Justify the payment of BAC Honoraria taken from the General Fund and Trust Fund by showing that these are within the guidelines of DBM Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007, otherwise, refund should be made by the concerned BAC Chair and Members.

In cases of deficiency in collections from the sources identified, the amount of honoraria shall be adjusted proportionately for all those BAC entitled thereto.

Follow strictly the provisions mandated under DBM-BC No. 2007-3 dated November 24, 2007.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to record the real property tax collections in the General and Special Education Fund Cash books, indicating the proceeds and/or sharing of basic real property tax and the additional 1% real property tax for the SEF.

Likewise, it is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should take up the journal entry of Collection indicating among others the proceeds of real property tax collections that will accrue to the intended accounts and/or beneficiaries. The same accounting entry will be applied to real property tax collections paid in advance and discounted.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Direct the Budget Officer to clearly define, prioritize and implement infrastructure projects charged to 20% Development Fund based on approved budget. Otherwise, ineligible claims will be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented
